ocr.text: The following table provides a comprehensive overview of various educational programs implemented in Kenya, focusing on
metrics such as the number of graduates and current trainees. Notably, the data reveals significant insights into the long-term
sustainability and impact of these programs since their inception. For instance, the program that commenced in 2004 has
produced a total of 20 graduates, while currently supporting 12 trainees. This reflects a commitment to in-country training and
mentorship, which are crucial for fostering local talent and expertise.
Program  Year Started  Graduates  Current Trainees  Total Enrollment
South Africa  2007  0  15  15
Nigeria  2008  0  8  8
Tanzania  2008  0  13  13
Rwanda  2010  0  4  4
WAFELTP*  2010  0  3  3
Totals  20  55  75
Author/Year  In-Country Training  Long Term Commitment  Increase Impact  Mentorship  Preparation of Students
Leonard, 2002  x
Spiegel et al., 2004  x  x
Furin et al., 2006  x  x  x
Elit et al., 2011  x  x
Provenzano et al., 2010  x  x
Scarlett et al., 2011  x  x  x
Redwood-Campbell et al., 2011  x  x
Crump & Sugarman, 2010  x  x  x
Furthermore, the total enrollment figure of 32 indicates a robust interest in these educational initiatives, suggesting that they are
effectively addressing local needs. The inclusion of metrics like "Long Term Commitment" and "Increase Impact" underscores a
strategic approach to program development, emphasizing not just immediate educational outcomes but also the enduring
benefits for the community. As educational institutions continue to evolve in response to local contexts, the data serves as a
valuable resource for stakeholders aiming to enhance the preparation of students for future challenges. Overall, the trends
illustrated in this table highlight the critical role of targeted educational programs in advancing both individual and community
development in Kenya.
